instruments: Policy Statement 2.0 on the Development of Digital Assets in Hong Kong (the "LEAP" framework)
standing_summary: Policy Statement 2.0, issued 26 June 2025, set out a government-wide "LEAP" framework (Legal streamlining, Expanding tokenised products, Advancing use cases, People and partnership development) for digital-asset development. Tokenization activity has since moved from pilots to routine, real-value issuance: the HKSAR Government priced its third digital green bond in November 2025 (equivalent to roughly HK$10 billion across four currencies), the first bond reported to settle in both e-CNY and e-HKD tokenised central bank money. HKMA's Project Ensemble progressed to "EnsembleTX," a real-value pilot phase launched 13 November 2025 focused on tokenised-deposit settlement of tokenised money-market-fund transactions. On 20 April 2026, the SFC issued Circular 26EC22 formally permitting tokenisation of SFC-authorised investment products, including primary dealing and 24/7 secondary trading on licensed virtual asset trading platforms.

key_links.label: Government press release: Policy Statement 2.0 / LEAP framework (26 Jun 2025)
HKMA press release: Project Ensemble new phase, EnsembleTX (13 Nov 2025)
HKMA press release: third digital/tokenised green bond offering (11 Nov 2025)
SFC Circular 26EC22: tokenisation of SFC-authorised investment products (20 Apr 2026)

open_items: The EnsembleTX pilot is stated to run throughout 2026; its outputs and any expansion have not yet concluded.
The comprehensive legal review for real-world-asset tokenisation flagged in Policy Statement 2.0 has no discrete published output found yet.
Migration from interim RTGS settlement to 24/7 tokenised central-bank-money settlement under Project Ensemble is described as a future step, not yet completed.
